title: What does Misato think about A S
display_title: What does Misato think about A S
wikitext:
[]: What does Misato think about A/S?             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 12, 2009 12:25 pm It occurs to me that Misato actually seemed to be rooting for a Shinji/Rei hookup for most of the series, in opposition to Kaji's efforts to scrape Asuka's affections off on some other poor sap. Couple that with her own designs on the boy and her general tolerance of but not fondness for Asuka, and I have to wonder if she would have approved of such a relationship had it gone anywhere. Just curious what others think.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Sun Apr 12, 2009 1:47 pm Misato: No jailbait for me? She would be disappointed, but I think she would get over it, eventually.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Apr 12, 2009 1:54 pm I'd think she was up for S/A, otherwise she could have offered Asuka to move out with all the bickering once the israfel fight was over.

[]:             Quote Post by The Invincible Shinji » Sun Apr 12, 2009 2:40 pm I think Misato just wanted Shinji to be happy, regardless of wither it was Rei or Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 12, 2009 2:49 pm I don't know... her role in P3II seemed to me to contain a futile attempt to keep Asuka away from him. Asuka: Say... wanna kiss?.. Misato: You mustn't! Asuka: Or are you afraid? Misato: It's not something kids should do.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Apr 12, 2009 3:00 pm NemZ wrote: I don't know... her role in P3II seemed to me to contain a futile attempt to keep Asuka away from him. Asuka: Say... wanna kiss?.. Misato: You mustn't! Asuka: Or are you afraid? Misato: It's not something kids should do.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 12, 2009 3:03 pm Shin-seiki wrote: I would say that tends to indicate that Misato was sort of oblivious to what was going on with Shinji and Asuka,

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Apr 12, 2009 3:13 pm NemZ wrote: I would agree with you except that this is happing within a moment of instrumentality, thus she should know what's up... right?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 12, 2009 3:18 pm true, it is pretty early in the sequence.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 12, 2009 3:27 pm Shin-seiki wrote: I would say that tends to indicate that Misato was sort of oblivious to what was going on with Shinji and Asuka

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 12, 2009 4:08 pm I don't think Misato would care who Shinji was with (though she wants in on some of that) , as long as he's happy. In regards to Shinji/Asuka specifically, she'd probably be surprised at first (given the fact she seems oblivious in ep9 and 15), but I'm sure she'd be rooting for them soon enough. Although I do wonder if she was going along with Kaji's "scheme" in episode 9. With Misato's fondness or lack thereof for Asuka, I figured that Misato was making an attempt to be there for her (partly why Misato had Asuka move in?), but Asuka's stubborn personality just made things worse with their relationship. One thing I gathered from Misato with Eva is that she does care for all of the pilots, it's just Shinji's her "favorite". For various reasons. >_>;; And since this is related to the topic, when Asuka was growing up in Germany, she WAS living with her dad and stepmother, yes? I know some fans like to think that Misato and later Kaji were her actual guardians, but I figured they only helped her with Nerv related matters and at home she'd just live with/ignore Langley and her stepmom.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Apr 12, 2009 4:10 pm I never realized the pairing was all Kaji's fault but now it makes perfect sense. Having to dance to kill an angel, yea right, obviously had ulterior motives

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 12, 2009 4:21 pm The theory comes from an old ANF discussion. BUT there is some merit to the fact Kaji seems to divert Asuka's attention from himself long enough that she notices Shinji...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 12, 2009 4:53 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Although I do wonder if she was going along with Kaji's "scheme" in episode 9.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 12, 2009 6:15 pm NemZ wrote: Was she seriously considering making the switch if Shinji and Asuka didn't come back together united in purpose?

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Sun Apr 12, 2009 7:42 pm I don't think she really thought about that sort of thing. It seems to me she thought kids dont really get involved in that stuff. She never seemed to notice Asuka was sad, although Rei and Shinji clearly are.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 13, 2009 1:54 am When she was Asuka's age, Misato definitely wasn't getting involved in that stuff. But then part of the point of the series is that breakdown in communication between people.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Apr 13, 2009 2:18 am Perhaps Misato's different reaction is because with Rei she thinks it would be a cute and relatively harmless arrangement, while with Asuka (especially noting her Kaji glomping) the situation is likely to get serious, and quickly.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Apr 13, 2009 11:16 am Asuka is obviously sexualized and Misato probably a) sees her as on the verge of [at least a kind of] womanhood; and b) subconsiously as competition for Shinji. Rei is obviously not sexualized and Misato probably sees her as a good [at least for a while] platonic playdate for Shinji - and a part of normal growing up for the "damaged" boy. Perhaps this will form the core of a corps, and might provide now and future stability for the project and for Shinji. Misato has difficulty with her subconscious attraction to Shinji, and she balances things by holding Shinji as a future possibility for herself, while recognizing that despite the age of consent he is not capable of informed consent. As another threa a while back discussed, there seems evidence of Misato's confusion over what role she has/wants in Shinji's life. I think Anno consciously uses this age range to look at that cusp of cultural - biological - psychological coming of age. Misato, despite her formal position, seems emotionally the same age as her "wards".

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Apr 13, 2009 12:42 pm I always got the impression that Misato knew they would clash and thought it would be amusing (in addition to being a convenient arrangement for two pilots, of course).
